question: What is the average of competitors with no weight?  average = DIVIDE(COUNT(person.id), SUM(weight = 0)); no weight refers to weight WHERE person.weight = 0;
orig_question: What is the average of superheroes with no skin colour?  average = DIVIDE(COUNT(superhero.id), SUM(skin_colour_id = 1)); no skin colour refers to skin_colour_id WHERE colour.id = 1;
orig_masked: What is the average of [T9] with no [C27]?  average = DIVIDE(COUNT([T9].[C6]), SUM([C27] = [V1])); no [C27] refers to [C27] WHERE [C6] = [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C6]
evidence: average = DIVIDE(COUNT(person.id), SUM(weight = 0)); no weight refers to weight WHERE person.weight = 0;
orig_evidence: average = DIVIDE(COUNT(superhero.id), SUM(skin_colour_id = 1)); no skin colour refers to skin_colour_id WHERE colour.id = 1;
gold_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN weight = 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM person
orig_gold_sql: SELECT CAST(COUNT(*) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T2.id = 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) FROM superhero AS T1 INNER JOIN colour AS T2 ON T1.skin_colour_id = T2.id

question: State the author name and publisher name of book ID '16'. When was the book published and what language does the book had.  When was the book published refers to `publication date`
orig_question: State the sex and birthday of patient ID '163109'. When was the examination taken and what symptom does the patient had.  When was the examination taken refers to `Examination Date`
orig_masked: State the [C64] and [C59] of [C63] '[V1]'. When was the [T1] [T1] and what [C12] does the [T3] had.  When was the [T1] [T1] refers to `[C7]`; [V1] is a value of the column [C63]
evidence: When was the book published refers to `publication date`
orig_evidence: When was the examination taken refers to `Examination Date`
gold_sql: SELECT T3.author_name, T4.publisher_name, T1.publication_date, T5.language_name FROM book as T1 INNER JOIN book_author as T2 ON T1.book_id = T2.book_id INNER JOIN author T3 ON T2.author_id = T3.author_id INNER JOIN publisher as T4 ON T1.publisher_id = T4.publisher_id INNER JOIN book_language AS T5 ON T1.language_id = T5.language_id WHERE T1.book_id = 16
orig_gold_sql: SELECT T1.SEX, T1.Birthday, T2.`Examination Date`, T2.Symptoms FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Examination AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T1.ID = 163109

question: What is the genre of the newest movie? genre refers to movie.genre; newest movie refers to latest release date;
orig_question: What is the preferred foot when attacking of the youngest football player? preferred foot when attacking refers to preferred_foot; youngest football player refers to latest birthday;
orig_masked: What is the [C159] of the [C121] [T4]? [C159] refers to [C159]; [C121] [T4] refers to [C121] [C121];
evidence: genre refers to movie.genre; newest movie refers to latest release date
orig_evidence: preferred foot when attacking refers to preferred_foot; youngest football player refers to latest birthday;
gold_sql: SELECT genre from movie ORDER BY "Release Date" DESC LIMIT 1
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.preferred_foot FROM Player AS t1 INNER JOIN Player_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.player_api_id = t2.player_api_id ORDER BY t1.birthday DESC LIMIT 1

question: Indicate the car names of the top 10 cars with the highest number of horsepower. car name refers to car_name; cars with highest number of horsepower refers to MAX(horsepower);
orig_question: Indicate the full names of the top 10 players with the highest number of penalties. full name refers to player_name; players with highest number of penalties refers to MAX(penalties);
orig_masked: Indicate the [C126] of the top 10 [T4] with the highest number of [C154]. [C126] refers to [C126]; [T4] with highest number of [C154] refers to MAX([C154]);
evidence: car name refers to car_name; cars with highest number of horsepower refers to MAX(horsepower);
orig_evidence: full name refers to player_name; players with highest number of penalties refers to MAX(penalties);
gold_sql: SELECT car_name FROM data ORDER BY horsepower DESC LIMIT 10
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.player_name FROM Player_Attributes AS t1 INNER JOIN Player AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.id ORDER BY t1.penalties DESC LIMIT 10

question: For the business "Cable Car Corner", what was its inspection score on 2014-09-02? "Cable Car Corner" refers to business.name = 'Cable Car Corner'; on 2014-09-02 refers to inspection.date LIKE '2014-09-02%';
orig_question: For the team "Hannover 96", what was its defence aggression class on 2015/9/10? "Hannover 96" refers to team_long_name = 'Hannover 96'; on 2015/9/10 refers to date LIKE '2015-09-10%';
orig_masked: For the [T6] "[V1]", what was its [C188] on [V3]? "[V1]" refers to [C173] = '[V1]'; on [V3] refers to [C189] LIKE '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C173]; [V2] is a value of the column [C189]; [V3] is a value of the column [C189]
evidence: "Cable Car Corner" refers to business.name = 'Cable Car Corner'; on 2014-09-02 refers to inspection.date LIKE '2014-09-02%';
orig_evidence: "Hannover 96" refers to team_long_name = 'Hannover 96'; on 2015/9/10 refers to date LIKE '2015-09-10%';
gold_sql: SELECT T1.score FROM inspections AS T1 INNER JOIN businesses AS T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T2.name = 'Cable Car Corner' AND T1.date = '2014-09-02'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t2.chanceCreationShootingClass FROM Team AS t1 INNER JOIN Team_Attributes AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id WHERE t1.team_long_name = 'Hannover 96' AND t2.`date` LIKE '2015-09-10%'

question: Among all females, list out those have low weight level. State the different IDs and their height. females refers to gender = 'F'; low weight level refers to weight < 35;
orig_question: Among all outpatients, list out those have low hemoglobin level. State the different IDs and their sex. outpatients refers to Admission = '-'; low hemoglobin level refers to HBG < 10;
orig_masked: Among all [C58], list out those have [C31]. State the different [C63] and their [C64]. [C58] refers to [C58] = '[V2]'; [C31] refers to HBG < [V1];; [V1] is a value of the column [C31]; [V2] is a value of the column [C58]
evidence: females refers to gender = 'F'; low weight level refers to weight < 35;
orig_evidence: outpatients refers to Admission = '-'; low hemoglobin level refers to HBG < 10;
gold_sql: SELECT id, height FROM person WHERE weight < 35 AND gender = 'F'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID, T1.SEX FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.HGB < 10 AND T1.Admission = '-'
note: Missing link to HBG filled in

question: For the competitors with the normal weight level, how many of them are American? normal weight level refers to weight < 100; American refers to noc_region.region_name = 'USA';
orig_question: For the patients with the normal glutamic pylvic transaminase level, how many of them are male? normal glutamic pylvic transaminase level refers to GOT < 60; male refers to Sex = 'M';
orig_masked: For the [T3] with the [C28], how many of them are [C64]? [C28] refers to [C28] < [V1]; [C64] refers to [C64] = '[V2]';; [V1] is a value of the column [C28]; [V2] is a value of the column [C64]
evidence: normal weight level refers to weight < 100; American refers to noc_region.region_name = 'USA';
orig_evidence: normal glutamic pylvic transaminase level refers to GOT < 60; male refers to Sex = 'M';
gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT T1.ID) FROM person as T1 INNER JOIN person_region as T2 ON T2.person_id = T1.id INNER JOIN noc_region AS T3 ON T3.id = T2.region_id WHERE T3.region_name = 'USA' AND T1.weight < 100
orig_gold_sql: SELECT COUNT(T1.ID) FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.GOT < 60 AND T1.SEX = 'M'

question: Does the AFC SUSHI @ MOLLIE STONE'S 2 business have a low, moderate or high risk category? AFC SUSHI @ MOLLIE STONE'S 2 refers to businesses.name = 'AFC SUSHI @ MOLLIE STONE'S 2'; risk category refers to risk_category
orig_question: Does the KSV Cercle Brugge team have a slow, balanced or fast speed class? KSV Cercle Brugge refers to team_long_name = 'KSV Cercle Brugge'; speed class refers to buildUpPlaySpeedClass
orig_masked: Does the [V1] [T6] have a [C181]? [V1] refers to [C173] = '[V1]'; speed class refers to [C181]; [V1] is a value of the column [C173]
evidence: AFC SUSHI @ MOLLIE STONE'S 2 refers to businesses.name = 'AFC SUSHI @ MOLLIE STONE'S 2'; risk category refers to risk_category
orig_evidence: KSV Cercle Brugge refers to team_long_name = 'KSV Cercle Brugge'; speed class refers to buildUpPlaySpeedClass
gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T2.risk_category FROM businesses as T1 INNER JOIN violations as T2 ON T1.business_id = T2.business_id WHERE T1.name = 'AFC SUSHI @ MOLLIE STONE''S 2'
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT t1.buildUpPlaySpeedClass FROM Team_Attributes AS t1 INNER JOIN Team AS t2 ON t1.team_api_id = t2.team_api_id WHERE t2.team_long_name = 'KSV Cercle Brugge'
note: Added in link to speed class

question: Give the name of the division had the most matches in the 2016 season? division that had the most matches in the 2016 season refers to MAX(divisions.name WHERE season = '2016');
orig_question: Give the name of the league had the most matches in the 2008/2009 season? league that had the most matches in the 2008/2009 season refers to MAX(league_name WHERE season = '2008/2009');
orig_masked: Give the [C5] of the [T2] had the most [T3] in the [V1] [C108]? [T2] that had the most [T3] in the [V1] [C108] refers to MAX([C5] WHERE [C108] = '[V1]');; [V1] is a value of the column [C108]
evidence: division that had the most matches in the 2016 season refers to MAX(divisions.name WHERE season = '2016');
orig_evidence: league that had the most matches in the 2008/2009 season refers to MAX(league_name WHERE season = '2008/2009');
gold_sql: SELECT T2.name FROM matchs AS T1 INNER JOIN divisions AS T2 ON T1.div = T2.division WHERE season = '2016' GROUP BY Div HAVING COUNT(*) = (SELECT COUNT(div) FROM matchs WHERE season = '2016' GROUP BY Div ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC LIMIT 1)
orig_gold_sql: SELECT t1.name FROM League AS t1 JOIN Match AS t2 ON t1.id = t2.league_id WHERE t2.season = '2008/2009' GROUP BY t1.name HAVING COUNT(t2.id) = (SELECT MAX(match_count) FROM (SELECT COUNT(t2.id) AS match_count FROM Match AS t2 WHERE t2.season = '2008/2009' GROUP BY t2.league_id))

question: Provide all model year, country name and mpg of vehicles whose displacement just within the borderline of passing? displacement just within the borderline of passing refers to displacement = 70;
orig_question: Provide all ID, sex and birthday of patients whose urea nitrogen (UN) just within the borderline of passing? urea nitrogen (UN) just within the borderline of passing refers to UN = 29;
orig_masked: Provide all [C63], [C64] and [C59] of [T3] whose [C56] [C56]? [C56] [C56] refers to [C56] = [V1]; ; [V1] is a value of the column [C56]
evidence: displacement just within the borderline of passing refers to displacement = 70;
orig_evidence: urea nitrogen (UN) just within the borderline of passing refers to UN = 29;
gold_sql: SELECT T2.model_year, T3.origin, T1.mpg FROM data as T1 INNER JOIN production AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID INNER JOIN country AS T3 ON T2.country = T3.origin WHERE T1.displacement = 70
orig_gold_sql: SELECT DISTINCT T1.ID, T1.SEX, T1.Birthday FROM Patient AS T1 INNER JOIN Laboratory AS T2 ON T1.ID = T2.ID WHERE T2.UN = 29
